questions.q: Jahangir was a ruler of which Indian empire?
questions.explanation: Jahangir was the fourth emperor of the Mughal Empire in India.

questions.q: Jahangir was the son and successor of which great Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Jahangir succeeded his father Akbar, the third Mughal emperor, in 1605.

questions.q: In which century did Jahangir reign as Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Jahangir reigned from 1605 to 1627 CE, placing his rule in the early 17th century.

questions.q: Jahangir is especially remembered as a patron of which art form?
questions.explanation: Jahangir was a noted connoisseur and patron of Mughal painting and the arts.

questions.q: An English ambassador, Sir Thomas Roe, visited Jahangir's court on behalf of which country?
questions.explanation: Sir Thomas Roe was an envoy from England who came to Jahangir's court to seek trading rights.

questions.q: The name 'Jahangir' is commonly translated as which of the following?
questions.explanation: The regnal title 'Jahangir' means 'Seizer' or 'Conqueror of the World'.

questions.q: What was the given birth name of the prince who became Emperor Jahangir?
questions.explanation: Before taking the throne he was Prince Salim, named in part after the Sufi saint Salim Chishti.

questions.q: Jahangir's most powerful and influential queen was which woman?
questions.explanation: Nur Jahan was Jahangir's chief consort and wielded great political influence at his court.

questions.q: Which European trading company gained important commercial privileges in India during Jahangir's reign?
questions.explanation: The English East India Company secured trading rights, aided by Sir Thomas Roe's embassy to Jahangir.

questions.q: Jahangir's son and successor, who built the Taj Mahal, was which emperor?
questions.explanation: Jahangir was succeeded by his son Shah Jahan, the builder of the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: In what year did Jahangir ascend the Mughal throne?
questions.explanation: Jahangir became emperor in 1605 CE upon the death of his father Akbar.

questions.q: Jahangir's memoirs, which record his observations and reign, are known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Tuzuk-i-Jahangiri, or Jahangirnama, is the emperor's own memoir.

questions.q: Early in his reign Jahangir faced a rebellion led by his own eldest son, who was which prince?
questions.explanation: Prince Khusrau Mirza rebelled against Jahangir in 1606 and was ultimately blinded after his revolt failed.

questions.q: Jahangir was said to have set up a 'chain of justice' (zanjir-i-adl) so petitioners could reach him. Where was it installed?
options: On a ship on the Yamuna
questions.explanation: Jahangir's famous golden chain of justice was hung at the Agra Fort so subjects could ring it for redress.

questions.q: Jahangir's reign saw the execution of a major Sikh religious figure. Which Guru was this?
questions.explanation: Guru Arjan, the fifth Sikh Guru, was executed under Jahangir in 1606, partly for aiding the rebel prince Khusrau.

questions.q: Jahangir's mother, a Rajput princess from Amber, is popularly remembered by which name?
questions.explanation: Jahangir's mother, often called Jodha Bai or Mariam-uz-Zamani, was a Rajput princess of Amber married to Akbar.

questions.q: Which Mughal province in the Deccan did Jahangir's forces struggle to fully subdue against the resistance of Malik Ambar?
questions.explanation: The Abyssinian general Malik Ambar of Ahmadnagar long frustrated Jahangir's campaigns in the Deccan.

questions.q: Jahangir greatly favoured which northern region as a retreat, lavishing gardens upon it?
questions.explanation: Jahangir loved Kashmir, where he and Nur Jahan laid out famous Mughal gardens such as Shalimar Bagh.

questions.q: Jahangir's keen interest in natural history is reflected in his patronage of which celebrated painter?
questions.explanation: Court artists such as Ustad Mansur and Bichitr produced detailed studies of birds, animals, and portraits for the connoisseur emperor.

questions.q: In which year did Jahangir die, ending his roughly 22-year reign?
questions.explanation: Jahangir died in 1627 CE, after which his son Shah Jahan secured the throne.

questions.q: Jahangir's tomb stands in which city?
questions.explanation: Jahangir was buried at Shahdara near Lahore, where his garden tomb still stands.

questions.q: During Jahangir's reign the rebellious prince Khurram later took which regnal name as emperor?
questions.explanation: Prince Khurram, who rebelled in Jahangir's last years, became Emperor Shah Jahan in 1628.

questions.q: Which powerful noble briefly held Jahangir captive in 1626 during a rebellion?
questions.explanation: The general Mahabat Khan staged a coup in 1626 and held Jahangir prisoner, though Nur Jahan helped engineer his release.

questions.q: Jahangir granted the English permission to establish a 'factory' (trading post) at which western Indian port?
questions.explanation: Following Roe's embassy, the English East India Company was permitted a factory at Surat in Gujarat.

questions.q: Nur Jahan's father, who served as Jahangir's chief minister, held which title?
questions.explanation: Mirza Ghiyas Beg, Nur Jahan's father, bore the title Itimad-ud-Daula and served as Jahangir's wazir.

questions.q: Jahangir's allegorical court paintings often depicted him doing what, symbolising his vision of kingship?
options: Embracing rival Shah Abbas of Persia
questions.explanation: Idealised paintings showed Jahangir embracing the Safavid Shah Abbas or shooting symbolic enemies, projecting an image of universal sovereignty.

questions.q: Jahangir lost the strategically important fortress of Kandahar during his reign to which power?
questions.explanation: Safavid Persia under Shah Abbas captured Kandahar from the Mughals in 1622, a notable setback for Jahangir.

questions.q: The Rajput state of Mewar finally made peace with the Mughals under Jahangir in 1615, when which Rana submitted?
questions.explanation: Rana Amar Singh of Mewar, son of Maharana Pratap, concluded a treaty with Jahangir's son Prince Khurram in 1615.

questions.q: Jahangir's coins struck during Nur Jahan's ascendancy were notable because they bore whose name alongside or instead of his?
questions.explanation: Coins were issued in Nur Jahan's name during Jahangir's later years, a striking sign of her political power.

questions.q: Which Jain or zodiac-themed series of coins did Jahangir famously issue, breaking with orthodox convention?
questions.explanation: Jahangir issued an unusual series of gold and silver zodiac coins, each depicting a sign of the zodiac, reflecting his personal tastes.
